originalTitle: Nigeria to seek compensation for property abandoned by citizens fleeing South Africa
title: Helping Our Friends: Nigeria Asks About Things Left Behind
content:
Hello, hello, my wonderful friends all over Africa! It's your friendly news reporter, here to share some important news in a super simple way. Today, we're talking about two amazing countries in Africa: Nigeria and South Africa. They are like big neighbors in our beautiful continent. Our news today is about Nigeria helping its people who used to live in South Africa.

A Big Trip Home

Imagine you live in a different town for a little while, and then one day, you decide it's time to go back to your very own home. That's a bit like what happened! Some people from Nigeria were living and working in South Africa. They had jobs, homes, and friends there. But then, for different reasons, they felt worried and decided it was best to go back home to Nigeria. It was a big trip for them and their families. When you move from one place to another, sometimes it's hard to take everything with you. Especially if you have to pack up quickly! These Nigerian friends had to make a big journey back to their home country. They wanted to be safe and with their families. Did you know? Many people in the world live in different countries for a while! It's like going on a very long adventure, but sometimes you miss home.

Leaving Things Behind

Now, imagine you have a favorite toy, or your family has a shop where they sell yummy snacks. If you had to leave your house in a hurry, you might not be able to take your toy or your shop with you. That's exactly what happened to some of these people. They had houses, shops, cars, and many other things they owned in South Africa. We call these things 'property' or 'businesses'. These things were very important to them. Their shops helped them earn money to buy food and clothes. Their homes were where they slept and played. But when they left to go back to Nigeria, many of these things were left behind. They couldn't just put a whole house in their backpack, could they? It can be sad to leave important things behind, especially when you worked hard for them. Nigeria wants to make sure its people are taken care of, even when they're far from home.

What is Nigeria Doing?

So, what is Nigeria doing to help? Nigeria has a special helper, like a super-ambassador, in South Africa. This special helper is called a 'High Commissioner'. Imagine he is Nigeria's best friend and message-giver in South Africa. His job is to look after Nigerians who are there and to talk to the government of South Africa. Our news tells us that this special helper, Nigeria's High Commissioner, is making a very important list. He is writing down all the shops, houses, and other things that were left behind by the Nigerians who went home. We call this 'documenting'. It means writing down every detail carefully, like when you list all your toys before cleaning your room. Did you know? Countries have special helpers called ambassadors or high commissioners in many other countries! They help their people and make sure countries can talk to each other like good neighbors.

Why is Making a List Important?

Why is this list so important? Well, imagine if you lost your favorite drawing, and you told your parents, 'I lost something!' They might ask, 'What did you lose? What did it look like?' The list helps Nigeria know exactly what was left behind. It helps them understand how many shops, how many houses, and what other things belong to their people. This list is like proof. It helps Nigeria to talk to South Africa and say, 'Look, our people had these things. They worked hard for them.' It's the first big step to helping people get what is fair.

What is Compensation?

Now, for a big word: 'compensation'. Don't worry, it's not as tricky as it sounds! Compensation means getting something back for what you lost. Imagine if your friend accidentally broke your crayon. A good friend might give you a new crayon, or help you fix the old one. That's a bit like compensation. Nigeria is hoping to get compensation for its people. This could mean getting money for the things they lost, or perhaps finding a way for them to get their property back. It's about being fair and helping people start again after a difficult time. It's like when you lose a toy, and someone helps you find it or get a new one so you can play again. Did you know? When something unfair happens and people lose things, countries often try to find a way to make things fair again. This helps everyone feel respected.

Countries Helping Each Other

Nigeria and South Africa are two big and important countries in Africa. It's like having two big brothers in a family. Sometimes, big brothers need to talk things out, especially when one's friends are worried. By making this list and talking, Nigeria is showing how much it cares about its people. It's like a parent always looking out for their children, no matter where they are. Countries in Africa often work together and help each other. We are all part of the big African family! This news shows that Nigeria is working hard to help its citizens who came back home, making sure they get a fair chance to rebuild their lives.

What Happens Next?

So, what will happen next? First, Nigeria's special helper will finish making that important list of all the things left behind. Then, Nigeria will talk to South Africa, sharing this list and discussing how they can help the people who returned home. They will work together, like solving a puzzle, to find the best way forward. It might take a little time, but it's important work. Nigeria wants to make sure its people who came back feel safe and that they can get help for what they lost. It's all about helping our neighbors and friends feel good and secure. We'll keep you updated on what happens next in this important story. Until then, remember to always be kind to your neighbors, just like countries are trying to be with each other!
summary: Nigeria is making a list of homes and shops left behind by its citizens who returned from South Africa to ask for fair help.
whyMatters: This is important because it shows how countries like Nigeria help their people, making sure they are treated fairly and can get back things they lost when they had to leave another country.

originalTitle: Sudan's RSF committed crimes against humanity in el-Fasher, Amnesty says
title: Sudan's RSF committed crimes against humanity in el-Fasher, Amnesty says
content: The Amnesty report adds to evidence of atrocities in el-Fasher, which the UN says bore the hallmarks of genocide.

originalTitle: Thousands of anti-migrant protesters march in South Africa amid heavy police presence
title: News from Sunny South Africa: Friends Are Moving
content:
Hello, my little news explorers! Reporter Kiki here, bringing you some news from a beautiful country called South Africa. Have you ever been to a big party or a school play where lots of people gather? Well, something like that happened recently, but it was a bit different. Many grown-ups in South Africa gathered and walked together to share a strong message. These grown-ups are called "protesters".

What Happened in Sunny South Africa?

Imagine your town or village. Now imagine some people from another village come to live there. They might come for new jobs, or maybe to be closer to family, or just for an adventure! In South Africa, many friends from other countries have come to live and work. We call these friends "migrants" or "foreigners." It just means they were born in a different country but now live in South Africa. Recently, some groups of people in South Africa, who we can call "local friends," felt very strongly that they wanted these friends from other countries to go back to their home countries. They organised a big walk, like a parade, to show how they felt. This is what we call a "protest." When people protest, they walk together, sometimes holding signs, to let everyone know their important message. **Did you know?** South Africa is famous for its amazing animals like lions, elephants, and rhinos! It's also home to Nelson Mandela, a very kind leader who taught everyone about peace and friendship. These local friends had set a special time, like a "deadline," saying by this time, they wished the friends from other countries would leave. It was like saying, "We wish you would pack your bags by next week!" Because of this, many friends who came from other countries decided it was time to go back to their own homes. They packed their bags and started their journeys back. It's a bit like when you have a sleepover at a friend's house, but then it's time to go back to your own bed. Sometimes it's sad, but sometimes it's also nice to go back to where you belong. People move for many different reasons. Maybe their mom or dad got a new job in a different town. Or maybe their family wants to live closer to their aunties and uncles. Sometimes, people move to a new place because they want to learn new things or have new adventures! And sometimes, they move because they want to feel safer. All these reasons are okay, and people often miss their old homes and friends when they move. **Did you know?** Africa is a giant continent with 54 different countries! Each country has its own special songs, stories, and delicious foods. Isn't that amazing?

Police Are There to Help Everyone

When lots of people gather for a big walk or a protest, it's very important to make sure everyone stays safe. That's where our wonderful police officers come in! In South Africa, during these big walks, there were many police officers present. Their job was to look after everyone, both the people protesting and anyone else around. They made sure that no one got hurt and that everything stayed peaceful and calm. Imagine your teacher helping everyone line up nicely and making sure no one pushes or shoves. That's a bit like what the police do in big crowds. They are there to keep order and protect people. **Did you know?** Police officers wear uniforms and often drive special cars or motorbikes to help them do their important job of keeping our communities safe. They are our helpers! So, while some people were walking to share their strong feelings, the police were busy making sure everyone was safe. It's really important for grown-ups to make sure things are done safely, especially when there are many people together.

Why Do People Move From Place to Place?

This news story is about people moving. Sometimes people move across a town, sometimes across a country, and sometimes even across many countries! It’s a big world out there, and people are always on the move. Think about birds that fly south for the winter and then fly back when it's warm. Or maybe your family goes on holiday to a new place. People are a bit like that too! **Did you know?** Humans have been moving from one place to another for thousands of years! Sometimes it's for food, sometimes for water, and sometimes just to explore new lands. When friends from other countries move, they often bring wonderful things with them – new foods to try, different kinds of music, and exciting stories from their homes. And when they go back, they take new stories and experiences from the place they visited. It’s like a big exchange of ideas and cultures! It's important to remember that everyone, no matter where they come from, deserves to be treated with kindness and respect. We should always try to be good neighbours and friends to everyone around us. So, in South Africa, some friends from other countries decided to move back home because of the big walks and the deadline. But everyone was safe, thanks to the grown-ups and the police who were watching over things. And people will continue to move and explore our big, beautiful world! **Did you know?** When people move to a new country, they often learn a new language! Imagine speaking two or even three languages – how clever is that?
summary: In South Africa, some local people walked together to ask friends from other countries to move back home, and many did, all while police helped keep everyone safe.
whyMatters: This matters because it shows how different groups of people can feel strongly about things, and it reminds us that being kind and safe is important for everyone, no matter where they live or come from.

originalTitle: Israel kills three Palestinians in Gaza despite ‘ceasefire’
title: News from Far Away: Hoping for Peace in Gaza
content:
Hello My Wonderful Friends!

Hello there, amazing explorers of the world! I am your friendly news reporter, and I am here in beautiful Africa. Today, I want to share some news with you. It comes from a place far, far away across the big, blue sea. This place is called Gaza. Sometimes, news can be a little bit sad. But even when things are sad, we can always hope for kindness and peace for everyone.

What Happened Far Away?

News has told us about something sad that happened in Gaza. There was a big noise in the sky. This noise came from a special flying machine. This flying machine is called a drone. Sadly, some people got hurt because of this. The news said three people were hurt. This happened near a very important building. This building is a hospital. Hospitals are very special places. They are where doctors and nurses help people. When someone is sick or gets hurt, they go to the hospital. It's a place for healing. The sad event also happened near a place where cars go. It's called a fuel station. Cars get special juice there to make them move. We feel sad when anyone gets hurt, especially near places meant for helping people or for everyday life.

Where is Gaza?

Gaza is a small strip of land. It is on the coast, right next to a big, blue sea. This sea is called the Mediterranean Sea. Imagine a long beach with many towns and homes along it. That's a little bit like Gaza. Many, many families live there. Children like you live and play in Gaza every day. It is a very busy place with lots of people.

Who Lives There?

In that part of the world, there are different groups of people. They live very close to each other. Sometimes, these groups have big disagreements. Like when you and a friend might argue about a toy. But sometimes, these arguments become very, very big. One group of people lives in an area often called Israel. Another group of people lives in an area often called Palestine, where Gaza is. Both groups have families and children who just want to be safe and happy.

What is a Drone?

Do you know what a toy airplane is? Or a little helicopter that can fly? Well, a drone is a bit like a bigger, very clever flying robot. Some drones are used for fun, to take pictures from high up. But some drones are used for other important jobs. And sometimes, they can make loud noises or cause trouble. The news told us that a drone was involved in the sad event near the hospital.

What is a Ceasefire?

The news also mentioned a special word: 'ceasefire.' What does 'ceasefire' mean? It's like a big promise. It's a promise that everyone will stop making loud noises. It means everyone promises to stop fighting for a while. Imagine when you and your friend have an argument. Then you both say, "Let's stop arguing now and be friends!" That's a little ceasefire. A big ceasefire means everyone hopes for peace and quiet. It is extra sad because this happened even when there was a promise to have a ceasefire. It means the promise was broken. We always hope that promises are kept, especially when they are about keeping people safe.

Why are Hospitals Super Special?

Hospitals are truly amazing places. They are safe zones. When someone feels poorly or gets an ouchie, they go to the hospital. Doctors and nurses work so hard there to make everyone feel better. They are heroes! Because hospitals are for helping and healing, no one should ever be hurt near one. Hospitals are places of hope. Everyone in the world agrees that hospitals should always, always be safe from any kind of loud noise or fighting.

What is a Fuel Station?

A fuel station is a normal place that many grown-ups go to. It is where cars, buses, and trucks get special liquid. This liquid makes them go zoom! It's like how you need your breakfast to have energy for playing. Cars need fuel to move around. It's part of everyday life. People go there to get ready for their journeys, not to be in danger.

Did You Know These Things?

Did you know that Gaza is one of the most crowded places in the world? Many, many people live in a small area there. Did you know that many children in Gaza love to play football? Just like many children here in Africa! Did you know that the Mediterranean Sea, which touches Gaza, is a beautiful sea that connects many different countries? Did you know that a ceasefire is meant to give people a chance to feel safe and happy, even for a little while?

Big Hopes for Peace

It is very, very sad when people get hurt. We always hope for peace for everyone. Peace means that children can play safely outside. Peace means that families can be together without worrying. Peace means no more loud noises that scare people. We wish for peace for all the children in Gaza. And for all the grown-ups too. We wish for peace for all people everywhere in the world. Let's all send our kind thoughts to the people in Gaza. Let's hope that everyone can be safe very soon. And that promises to be peaceful are always kept. Thank you for listening, my friends! See you next time with more news and hope for our world.
summary: News from a faraway place called Gaza tells us that some people sadly got hurt near a hospital, even when there was a promise to stop fighting, and we hope for peace for everyone there.
whyMatters: It matters because everyone, especially children, deserves to be safe and live in peace, and hospitals should always be safe places to help people get better.
